full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Figuring out what counts as a paper-specific assumption vs a general one.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: my exam says assumptions must be specific to the paper not like 'data is iid'. i found centering is needed but is that really specific to this paper or is centering just always good practice for SVMs?
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — clarification only. Wrote all assumption text myself.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified that in standard SVM, b compensates for non-centered data so centering is optional. Here b doesn't exist, so centering becomes structurally required — that makes it paper-specific.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Found the normalization recommendation in Section 1 of the paper.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: ok i have centering as assumption 1. what are 2 more assumptions that are actually in this specific paper not just general svm stuff
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — brainstorming. I picked from the suggestions and wrote everything myself.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested: valid Mercer kernel (for concavity of dual) and finite C (for convergence theorem). I then found the paper references for each myself.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Read Theorem 1 Section 5 and Section 2 dual derivation.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: what does PSD even mean for a kernel matrix, like in simple terms
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — just needed to understand the concept before writing about it.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Explained PSD = all eigenvalues non-negative = the matrix is like a 'bowl' shape in all directions. For SVMs this means the dual objective is concave = unique maximum.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Made sense with what I knew about convex optimization.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking whether the iteration bound in Theorem 1 actually depends on C.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: does theorem 1 in the paper actually have C in the iteration bound or am i misreading it
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed yes, the bound is proportional to C. So large C = potentially many more iterations.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Re-read Theorem 1 myself to confirm before adding this as an assumption.

top_5_prompts.prompt: is centering really specific to this paper or is it just general svm good practice?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This framing question was important — without it I might have written a generic assumption that the grader would reject.

top_5_prompts.prompt: ok i have centering as assumption 1, what are 2 more that are actually in this specific paper
top_5_prompts.why_important: Helped me identify the Mercer kernel and finite-C assumptions, both of which are traceable to specific theorems.

top_5_prompts.prompt: does theorem 1 actually have C in the iteration bound or am i misreading it
top_5_prompts.why_important: Needed to confirm the paper reference before writing the assumption — citing the wrong section would be a problem.

top_5_prompts.prompt: what does PSD even mean for a kernel matrix in simple terms
top_5_prompts.why_important: Had to understand it properly to explain why it matters for this method specifically.
